title: merge=union for JSONL gitattributes
rationale: Built-in git merge strategy that keeps all unique lines from both sides. Perfect for append-only JSONL — no custom merge driver needed unlike Beads which requires merge=beads

title: Provider-optimized prime formats
rationale: XML for Claude (40% accuracy variance on delimiter choice per research), plain text for Codex, markdown default. --format flag on mulch prime selects output.

description: Merge conflict resolved at tier ai-resolve. Branch: overstory/onboard-messaging-fixer/mulch-eb7c. Agent: onboard-messaging-fixer. Conflicting files: src/utils/format.ts.

name: type-registry
description: Built-in record types live behind TypeDefinition in src/registry/. TypeRegistry.get(name) returns the def; format.ts and expertise.ts iterate registry.enabled() instead of switching on r.type. Singleton via getRegistry() (lazy-falls-back to buildBuiltinRegistry); cli.ts calls setRegistry() at startup. Phase 2 of mulch-632e widens this with config-driven custom types.

title: pino is mulch's diagnostic channel, not the product-output channel
rationale: src/log.ts wires pino as a DIAGNOSTICS-only logger: writes to stderr (fd 2) so it never collides with the CLI result on stdout (markdown/--json results); routine internals log at debug (gated behind MULCH_DEBUG) so default runs stay silent. In --json mode diagnostic log lines are skipped/debug-gated because stderr must carry ONLY the machine-readable error object — a pino line there corrupts consumers that JSON.parse it (this regressed test/commands/registry-init-errors.test.ts until guarded). Product output keeps going through console.log/outputJson; pino is NOT a parallel product logger. Wired into src/utils/hooks.ts (exec trace at debug, failure/timeout at warn, outside the write lock) and the cli.ts registry-init catch.
